note: v2 honors the ECMAScript directive prologue — 'use client' is recognized past leading comments/blank lines and injected imports always land below the directive (shed 2026-07-13, extract-quirk-shed inc 03); v1 retains the byte-offset-0 quirk (transform_emitter directive check) until retirement, so its imports land above the comment-preceded directive, breaking Next.
v1 strip_consumed_imports is line-based and cannot strip multi-line named imports. v2 must reproduce until flipped.
v1 decides import emission by substring-grep of generated replacement text; user strings containing e.g. 'transforms.' trigger spurious imports. v2 must reproduce until flipped.

Duplicate identical compose() calls in one file: v1 matches families by slot structure with find() (lib.rs:1027), double-replacing the FIRST span with undefined mangling; v2 replaces each family at its own facts span, and its emitter errors loudly on overlapping edits where v1 silently skips out-of-range spans. v2 behavior is correct; divergence only manifests on a duplicate-compose unit (none in corpus yet). ACTIVATED at row 13 by the duplicate-compose.tsx fixture: v1 emits the FamA replacement at both spans (mangled), v2 emits each family at its own span. v2 is correct; flip sheds this entry.

selectorOrder is dead plumbing in v1's engine: both NAPI entry points parse it into an underscore-discarded binding (lib.rs:113, lib.rs:859) while SystemBuilder advertises and the plugins thread it. v2 deliberately has no such input. If v1 ever wires it, v2 must follow; until then the differential is symmetric because BOTH engines ignore it.

Per-component custom dynamic slot entries: v1 iterates a HashMap values() (per-process order), v2 iterates sorted_ids; generate_custom_prop_css_impl total-sorts before emission so this is neutralized today (verified by the semantics reviewer). Recorded in case a future generator change becomes input-order-sensitive.
